title: cleome serrulata subsp. angusta
intro: Cleome serrulata subsp. angusta is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Cleomaceae family, commonly known as the white bee plant or white bee flower, characterized by its small, white or pale yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cleome serrulata subsp. angusta include its potential to treat various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antiseptic and analgesic properties. Herbal preparations of Cleome serrulata subsp. angusta are typically made from the leaves and flowers, which can be dried and infused in hot water or used in teas, salves, and other topical applications.
